plans.title: Small/Micro-Cap Intraday Breakout System
plans.subtitle: Separate cash-delivery system targeting higher-volatility small/micro-cap intraday breakouts (NOT an ORB extension)

sections.title: Why a separate system (not an ORB extension)
sections.body: Small/micro caps DO move 3-5% intraday vs large caps' 1-2% — the volatility opportunity is real. But adding them to ORB breaks every operational assumption: no F&O = no 5x MIS leverage, wide bid-asks bust the 0.2% LIMIT buffer, frequent circuit filters trap positions, and many 'breakouts' are news-driven (already gapped). A separate system with different rules is the correct frame.

items.label: Cash delivery only
items.body: Most small/micro caps are NOT in F&O. MIS leverage is 1x at best. No shorting overnight. No futures hedge. Position sizing changes from 1.5L notional (3L × 5x lev / 5 concurrent) down to ~50K cash per stock.

items.label: Wider entry buffer
items.body: ORB uses 0.2% LIMIT buffer above breakout — fills cleanly on RELIANCE. On a Rs 80 small cap with bid-ask of Rs 1, that buffer is 16 paise (won't fill). Use 1-2% LIMIT buffer or accept market orders with slippage budget.

items.label: Hard position cap (Rs 50K per stock)
items.body: On Rs 3L capital, max 6 concurrent at Rs 50K each = full deployment. No leverage means a 3% gap-down = Rs 1,500 loss per position vs ORB's Rs 3K risk per trade design — keeps daily-loss math sane.

items.label: Skip stocks with circuit history
items.body: Pre-filter universe: any stock that hit upper/lower circuit ≥3 times in last 60 trading days is excluded. Once a stock is in circuit, automated exits don't fire and position is stuck for the day.

items.label: Slower OR (30-min vs ORB's 15-min)
items.body: Small caps need more time to establish a clean range. 15-min OR on illiquid names captures noise. 30-min OR (09:15 to 09:45) gives a more meaningful range and filters opening-print weirdness on thin books.

items.label: Tighter universe filter (turnover floor)
items.body: Min average daily turnover Rs 50 Cr over last 30 trading days. Filters out genuine micro-caps where execution is impossible. Sweet spot is Rs 50-500 Cr daily turnover — high enough to fill, low enough to retain volatility edge.

items.label: OR window
items.body: 09:15 → 09:45 (30 minutes). After 09:45, OR_high and OR_low are locked.

items.label: Breakout trigger
items.body: 5-min candle CLOSE above OR_high (LONG) or below OR_low (SHORT). Same close-based confirmation as ORB to avoid wick-driven false breakouts.

items.label: Filters (which to keep, which to drop vs ORB)
items.body: Keep: VWAP alignment, RSI not extreme, gap filter (skip if gap > 2%). Drop: CPR width filter (small caps almost always have 'wide CPR' due to higher daily ATR pct, so this filter would over-reject).

items.label: Last entry time
items.body: 13:30 (vs ORB's 14:00) — give intraday move more room before 15:18 EOD squareoff. Small caps' afternoon volatility tends to be higher; tighter cutoff reduces over-trading.

items.label: Max 1 trade per stock per day
items.body: Same as ORB. No re-entries on the same name.

items.label: Stop loss
items.body: OR opposite edge (same as ORB). For LONG: stop = OR_low. For SHORT: stop = OR_high.

items.label: Target
items.body: 1.5R fixed target (same R:R as ORB's V0/V9 base). To be A/B tested vs 2R given small caps' wider intraday range.

items.label: Trail / lock
items.body: At 14:00 (1.25 hours before EOD): if in profit, move SL to entry + 0.5R (lock half the unrealized). If at loss, hold for time-stop. Same V9t_lock50 pattern as live ORB.

items.label: EOD squareoff
items.body: 15:18 hard exit (2 minutes before Zerodha's MIS auto-squareoff at 15:20).

items.label: Capital allocation
items.body: Rs 3L initial deployment (matches ORB scale). NO LEVERAGE — full Rs 3L is invested capital.

items.label: Risk per trade
items.body: 0.8% of capital = Rs 2,400 per trade. Quantity = floor(2400 / |entry - SL|). Same risk-anchor as ORB.

items.label: Max concurrent positions
items.body: 6 concurrent, Rs 50K notional cap per position. So max deployed = 6 × 50K = Rs 3L (full capital).

items.label: Daily loss limit
items.body: 3% of capital = Rs 9K. Soft block on new entries; force-close all on 1.5x = Rs 13.5K daily MTM loss.

sections.title: Execution challenges to solve before live
items: Bid-ask handling: real-time monitoring of spread vs 1% LIMIT buffer. Reject signals where spread > 0.5% (too thin to trade).
Slippage budget: 0.5% slippage assumption in backtest (vs ORB's ~0.05%). Stress test at 1% to confirm edge survives.
Circuit detection: real-time check on entry — if stock hit 4% intraday move already and approaching circuit limit, skip the signal.
Data quality: Kite 5-min bars on small caps can have gaps when liquidity is thin. Backtest must handle bar gaps without inventing prices.
Notification noise: small-cap universe of 50-100 stocks can produce 10+ candidate signals/day. Prioritization (turnover, ATR pct, sector concentration) needed.

items.label: Step 1 — base universe
items.body: Nifty Smallcap 250 + Nifty Microcap 250 = ~500 candidates. Excludes Nifty 50/Next 50/Midcap 150 (already in ORB territory).

items.label: Step 2 — turnover filter
items.body: Avg daily turnover Rs 50 Cr+ over last 30 trading days. Cuts to ~80-150 names.

items.label: Step 3 — circuit history filter
items.body: Exclude any name that hit upper/lower circuit ≥3 times in last 60 trading days. Cuts another 20-40.

items.label: Step 4 — backtest sweep
items.body: Run the 30-min OR breakout rules on remaining ~60-100 stocks for 2024-2026. Train/test split. Keep top 20-30 by per-stock PF and Sharpe.

items.label: Step 5 — paper trade 4-6 weeks
items.body: Live tick monitoring on the curated 20-30 names. Compare paper P&L distribution to backtest. Validate slippage and circuit assumptions.

sections.title: Backtest scope
sections.body: Period 2024-03-18 to 2026-03-12 (~500 trading days, matches existing 5-min data window). Bar TF: 5-min. Capital: Rs 3L cash. Costs: 0.5% round-trip (delivery STT + slippage). Walk-forward train 2024-Q2 to 2025-Q1, test 2025-Q2 to 2026-Q1.

sections.title: Pass criteria
items: OOS Profit Factor ≥ 1.20
OOS Sharpe ≥ 0.8
OOS MaxDD ≤ 15% (lower than ORB's 30% target — small caps demand tighter risk control)
Avg trade frequency: 2-4 trades/day (sweet spot for active management without overtrading)

plans.title: ORB Directional Options — Nifty
plans.subtitle: 5-min directional options overlay on the existing ORB cash signal

sections.title: Goal
sections.body: When the ORB signal fires on any of the 15 cash stocks, mirror that directional view on NIFTY via options. Exploit the already-computed ORB conviction to size an options trade without taking separate directional risk on the index. Paper-only until backtest confirms edge.

items.label: Variant A · Pure naked option sell
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY CE. On LONG signal → SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY PE.
pros: Collect full premium if direction is right
cons: Unbounded risk on the wrong-side tail

items.label: Variant B · Directional strangle
items.body: On SHORT signal → SELL closer-ATM CE + SELL far-OTM PE. On LONG signal → SELL closer-ATM PE + SELL far-OTM CE. Far-leg acts as tail hedge and improves margin.
pros: Lower net margin via spread benefit
Extra premium from the hedge leg

items.label: Variant C · Conviction-ranked router
items.body: Route grade A+/A signals to Variant A (naked — high conviction) · Route B/C to Variant B (hedged) · Skip anything with no stars.
pros: Uses existing conviction rubric already saved on positions
Right risk profile for each signal quality
cons: Needs sample size to confirm grade-vs-outcome correlation

items.label: 1. Hard SL at 2× entry premium
items.body: Classic short-premium rule. Max loss = initial premium collected. Place as exchange SL-L with trigger at 2× entry, limit at 2.1× entry.

items.label: 2. SuperTrend trail on NIFTY spot
items.body: Exit the option when NIFTY spot SuperTrend flips against the trade. Test ST(7, 2.0) and ST(7, 3.0) — narrower band (2.0) catches smaller reversals but may whipsaw.

items.label: 3. Time-based exit
items.body: Candidates: 14:30 (aggressive) · 15:15 (standard EOD) · 15:20 (Zerodha MIS auto-squareoff). Test whether 14:30 loses the late-afternoon intrinsic decay edge.

items.label: 4. Partial scale-out
items.body: Mirror the ORB R-multiple style: exit 50% of lots at +30% premium decay (i.e. option value drops to 0.7× entry) · let remainder ride till SL or ST-flip.

sections.title: Open design questions
items: Strike selection: fixed-offset from spot? LTP-based search targeting premium ~Rs 80? Delta-based (e.g. short the 0.30 delta)?
Capital allocation: 1 lot per ORB signal? Scaled by conviction grade? Capped by current MIS margin headroom?
Concurrent cap: how many parallel option positions? 1? 3? 5?
Expiry choice: current-week for max theta + max gamma, or next-week for smoother decay?
SL placement: exchange-side SL-L on the option leg, or monitor-driven 2× check every tick?
Direction conflict: what if two ORB stocks give opposite signals? Take both (opposing NIFTY options), net out, or pick by conviction?

sections.title: Prerequisites before backtesting
items: Need 20+ trading days of captured 1-min options data (currently 3 sessions)
Reconstruct 5-min NIFTY candles from 1-min data for the ST calculation
Wire the ORB signal bus to emit signals to a second consumer (options engine)
Build a simple backtester using the captured option_chain snapshots

sections.title: Integration notes
items: Reuses the existing ORB signal engine 1:1 — no new signal logic
Fires at the same moment the cash ORB trade fires (so same filters apply)
Conviction grade already saved on orb_positions — direct lookup
New DB: backtest_data/orb_options.db (positions / orders / pnl)
Keep in paper mode (nas-style) until backtest shows edge
